item.name: Modify
Factset Modify a Watchlist.

description.content: Modify a watchlist.
Added as a part of security scheme: basic

item.name: Factset Modify a Position in a Watchlist.

request.name: Factset Modify a Position in a Watchlist.

description.content:
Modify a position in a watchlist.

Certain error conditions yield errors as follows: 

|Error Condition|HTTP Error|
|-------|--------|
|At least one of the parameters `notation` or `comment` must be set.|400 Bad Request|
|The notation already exists in the watchlist.|400 Bad Request|
Added as a part of security scheme: basic

item.name: Positions
Factset Add a Position to a Watchlist.

request.name: Factset Add a Position to a Watchlist.

description.content:
Add a position to a watchlist.

Certain error conditions yield errors as follows: 

|Error Condition|HTTP Error|
|-------|--------|
|Maximum number of watchlist positions would exceed 100.|400 Bad Request|
|The watchlist does not exist.|400 Bad Request|
|The notation already exists in the watchlist.|400 Bad Request|
Added as a part of security scheme: basic

request.name: Factset Delete a Position of a Watchlist.

description.content: Delete a position of a watchlist.
Added as a part of security scheme: basic

request.name: Factset Details of the Position of a Watchlist.

Added as a part of security scheme: basic

item.name: Factset List of Positions of a Watchlist.

request.name: Factset List of Positions of a Watchlist.

description.content: List of positions of a watchlist.

query.description: Limit the attributes returned in the response to the specified set.
Sortable attributes. The sort order is ascending unless it is prefixed with a minus sign, in which case it is descending. A list of at most 2 (possibly prefixed) attribute name(s) is allowed.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: basic

info.name: FactSet Watchlist API for Digital Portals

description.content:
Watchlists keeps track of users_ individual investment objectives in
self-directed wealth management applications.
